title: Spaghetti Bolognese
description: A rich, slow-simmered meat sauce that clings to every strand of pasta. A family dinner staple.

steps.instruction: Heat olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add onion, carrot, and celery. Cook, stirring, for 8 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and tomato paste, cook 2 minutes.
Increase heat to medium-high. Add ground beef and pork. Cook, breaking up the meat, until browned. Drain excess fat.
Pour in the red wine and cook until reduced by half, about 3 minutes.
Add the crushed tomatoes. Stir to combine, then reduce heat to low. Partially cover and simmer for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the milk and simmer 10 more minutes. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Toss spaghetti with the sauce, adding pasta water as needed to loosen. Serve with freshly grated parmesan and fresh basil.

notes: The longer this simmers, the better it tastes. Makes great leftovers — the sauce can be made up to 3 days ahead.
